en: For this Melchizedek, king of Salem, priest of the Most High God, met Abraham returning from the slaughter of the kings and blessed him,
zh: 這麥基洗德就是撒冷王，又是至高 神的祭司。亞伯拉罕殺敗眾王回來的時候，麥基洗德迎接他，並且給他祝福。

en: and to him Abraham apportioned a tenth part of everything. He is first, by translation of his name, king of righteousness, and then he is also king of Salem, that is, king of peace.
zh: 亞伯拉罕也把自己得來的一切，拿出十分之一來給他。麥基洗德這名字翻譯出來，頭一個意思就是“公義的王”；其次是“撒冷王”，就是“平安的王”的意思。

en: He is without father or mother or genealogy, having neither beginning of days nor end of life, but resembling the Son of God he continues a priest forever.
zh: 他沒有父親，沒有母親，沒有族譜，也沒有生死的記錄，而是與 神的兒子相似，永遠作祭司。

en: See how great this man was to whom Abraham the patriarch gave a tenth of the spoils!
zh: 你們想一想這人是多麼偉大啊！祖先亞伯拉罕也要從上等的擄物中，拿出十分之一來給了他。

en: And those descendants of Levi who receive the priestly office have a commandment in the law to take tithes from the people, that is, from their brothers, though these also are descended from Abraham.
zh: 那些領受祭司職分的利未子孫，奉命按照律法向人民，就是自己的弟兄，收取十分之一；雖然他們都是出於亞伯拉罕的。

en: But this man who does not have his descent from them received tithes from Abraham and blessed him who had the promises.
zh: 可是那不與他們同譜系的麥基洗德，反而收納了亞伯拉罕的十分之一，並且給這蒙受應許的人祝福。

en: It is beyond dispute that the inferior is blessed by the superior.
zh: 向來都是位分大的給位分小的祝福，這是毫無疑問的。

en: In the one case tithes are received by mortal men, but in the other case, by one of whom it is testified that he lives.
zh: 在這裡，收取十分之一的，都是必死的；但在那裡，收納十分之一的，卻被證實是一位活著的。

en: One might even say that Levi himself, who receives tithes, paid tithes through Abraham,
zh: 並且可以這樣說，連那收取十分之一的利未，也透過亞伯拉罕繳納了十分之一。

en: for he was still in the loins of his ancestor when Melchizedek met him.
zh: 因為麥基洗德迎接亞伯拉罕的時候，利未還在他祖先的身體裡面。

en: Now if perfection had been attainable through the Levitical priesthood (for under it the people received the law), what further need would there have been for another priest to arise after the order of Melchizedek, rather than one named after the order of Aaron?
zh: 這樣看來，如果藉著利未人的祭司制度能達到完全的地步 ，為甚麼還需要照著麥基洗德的體系，另外興起一位祭司，而不照著亞倫的體系呢？

en: For when there is a change in the priesthood, there is necessarily a change in the law as well.
zh: 祭司的制度既然更改了，律法也必須更改。

en: For the one of whom these things are spoken belonged to another tribe, from which no one has ever served at the altar.
zh: 因為這些話所指的那位，原是屬於另外一個支派的，這支派向來沒有人在祭壇前供職。

en: For it is evident that our Lord was descended from Judah, and in connection with that tribe Moses said nothing about priests.
zh: 我們的主明明是從猶大支派出來的，關於這個支派，摩西並沒有提及祭司的事。

en: This becomes even more evident when another priest arises in the likeness of Melchizedek,
zh: 如果有另一位像麥基洗德那樣的祭司興起來，那麼，這裡所說的就更明顯了。

en: who has become a priest, not on the basis of a legal requirement concerning bodily descent, but by the power of an indestructible life.
zh: 他成了祭司，不是按著律法上肉身的條例，卻是按著不能毀壞的生命的大能。

en: For it is witnessed of him, "You are a priest forever, after the order of Melchizedek."
zh: 因為有為他作證的說： “你永遠作祭司， 是照著麥基洗德的體系。”

en: On the one hand, a former commandment is set aside because of its weakness and uselessness
zh: 一方面，從前的條例因為軟弱，沒有用處，就廢棄了；

en: (for the law made nothing perfect); but on the other hand, a better hope is introduced, through which we draw near to God.
zh: 另一方面，它卻帶來了更美的盼望，藉著這盼望，我們就可以親近 神。

en: And it was not without an oath. For those who formerly became priests were made such without an oath,
zh: 此外，還有關於誓言的事。其他成為祭司的，並不是用誓言立的；

en: but this one was made a priest with an oath by the one who said to him: "The Lord has sworn and will not change his mind, 'You are a priest forever.'"
zh: 只有耶穌是用誓言立的，因為那立他的對他說： “主已經起了誓， 決不改變， 你永遠作祭司。”

en: This makes Jesus the guarantor of a better covenant.
zh: 耶穌既然是用誓言立的，就成了更美好的約的保證。

en: The former priests were many in number, because they were prevented by death from continuing in office,
zh: 一方面，從前那些作祭司的，因為受死亡的限制，不能長久留任，所以人數眾多。

en: but he holds his priesthood permanently, because he continues forever.
zh: 另一方面，因為耶穌是永遠長存的，就擁有他永不更改的祭司職位。

en: Consequently, he is able to save to the uttermost those who draw near to God through him, since he always lives to make intercession for them.
zh: 因此，那些靠著他進到 神面前的人，他都能拯救到底；因為他長遠活著，為他們代求。

en: For it was indeed fitting that we should have such a high priest, holy, innocent, unstained, separated from sinners, and exalted above the heavens.
zh: 這樣的一位大祭司，對我們本是合適的。他是聖潔、沒有邪惡、沒有玷污、從罪人中分別出來、高過眾天的。

en: He has no need, like those high priests, to offer sacrifices daily, first for his own sins and then for those of the people, since he did this once for all when he offered up himself.
zh: 他不必像那些大祭司，天天先為自己的罪獻祭，然後為人民的罪獻祭；因為他獻上了自己，就把這事一次而永遠的成全了。

en: For the law appoints men in their weakness as high priests, but the word of the oath, which came later than the law, appoints a Son who has been made perfect forever.
zh: 律法所立的大祭司，都是軟弱的人；可是在律法以後，用誓言所立的兒子，卻是成為完全直到永遠的。
